text: Good — I have 15 merged PRs. Now let me filter to those with `closes_linear` set and fetch their Linear states:
Now I have 9 PRs closing Linear issues. Let me fetch all 9 issues' states in parallel:

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"
(def linear-states
  (zipmap (map second prs-with-linear)
          (map (comp :state linear__get_issue) (repeat {}) (map second prs-with-linear))))
linear-states
"}

message: unknown symbol 'repeat' — did you mean 'rest'?. Run (tables) to list your capabilities and (describe :name) for one of them.

text: `repeat` isn't available — let me build it differently:

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"
(def ids (map second prs-with-linear))
(def states (map #(linear__get_issue {:id %} :state) ids))
(def linear-states (zipmap ids states))
linear-states
"}

message: (linear__get_issue …) takes at most one argument map — e.g. (linear__get_issue {:arg value}). (describe :linear__get_issue) shows its parameters.

text: Right — the function only takes one arg map. Let me use reduce over a vector of results:

message: (linear__get_issue …) takes at most one argument map — e.g. (linear__get_issue {:arg value}). (describe :linear__get_issue) shows its parameters.

text: The function only accepts a single arg map. Let me wrap it in an anonymous fn and use `map` properly:

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"
(def ids (map second prs-with-linear))
(def states (map (fn [id] (:state (linear__get_issue {:id id}))) ids))
(def linear-states (zipmap ids states))
linear-states
"}

text: I have all 9 issue states. Now filter to those NOT equal to "done" and pair them with PR numbers:

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"
(def result
  (->> prs-with-linear
       (filter (fn [[pr linear-id]] (not= "done" (get linear-states linear-id))))
       (sort-by first)))
(count result)
(into {} result)
"}
